title: new pg Pool with ssl.rejectUnauthorized: false — the Postgres TLS certificate is not validated (MITM)

params.passDetail: The pg Pool validates the Postgres server certificate (ssl.rejectUnauthorized is not false) — the database connection's TLS identity is verified.
params.failDetail: new Pool is created with ssl: { rejectUnauthorized: false }, disabling validation of the Postgres server's TLS certificate. Anyone on the network path can present a forged certificate and man-in-the-middle the database connection — every query, credential, and row is exposed. Remove rejectUnauthorized: false; provide the managed provider's CA via ssl.ca instead.
params.absentCallDetail: No pg Pool construction in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: The pg Pool does not set ssl.rejectUnauthorized; the secure default (validate) applies.

provenance.note: ssl: { rejectUnauthorized: false } is the single most common node-postgres snippet on the internet, pasted to connect to Heroku/RDS without configuring the CA. It works, so it ships — silently disabling MITM protection on the database.
